entry.title: chave de acesso do modelo 91 (NFSE Nacional)
entry.content:
Estou com uma dúvida referente ao preenchimento padrão no SAP da chave de acesso do modelo 91 (NFSE Nacional).

Após a implementação das notas técnicas da SAP relacionadas à Reforma Tributária, já existe um standard definido para o preenchimento desse campo para fins fiscais?

Pergunto porque, para utilização do Beta da Apuração de CBS, o único parâmetro de comparação disponível na API é a chave de acesso de todos os modelos na base nacional.

Poderiam, por gentileza, confirmar se há alguma orientação oficial ou configuração recomendada?

entry.title: ‌🇧🇷‌ Webinar: 1st ASUG BR Meeting - SIG TDF & SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Operating in Brazil and planning a transition from Tax Declaration Framework (TDF) to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC)? Or following recent and upcoming changes related to SPED EFD‑Contributions or Layout 12 da ECF?

These ASUG‑hosted sessions provide an opportunity to gain practical insights into the latest Brazil localization developments and understand how SAP customers are addressing regulatory change within their landscapes.

Participants can expect:

  • An overview of key regulatory updates affecting Brazil localization
  • Guidance on transitioning from TDF to SAP DRC
  • Context on SPED EFD‑Contributions changes and Layout 12 da ECF
  • Exchange of experiences and best practices within the ASUG community

The sessions are designed for customers and partners who want to stay informed, assess impact, and prepare their systems and processes for upcoming compliance requirements in Brazil.

Speaker:

Eduardo Kyono, Local Product Manager, Brazil, SAP
 
Registration link

Are you interested in learning more about Tax Reform and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance from SAP experts? Join the following Work Zone groups to stay up to date:
  • Brazil - Tax Reform Localization
  • Brazil - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (EFD-REINF)
  • Brazil - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (NF-e)

entry.title: Re: Automatic Sales Order Creation From PO Data Which Ava...
entry.content:
Dear @Ashish13,

Option 1. Yes you can create Sales documents from PO PDFs

SAP provides standard apps to upload a customer PO PDF/image, extract fields, create a Sales Order Request and then convert it to a Sales Order:

  • Create Sales Orders Automatic Extraction (F4920), scope item 4X9 must be activated for the extraction scenario to be available in the system

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4920')/S35

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/2dcf49a616b842b096e0a3cad4dac458.html

  • Create Sales Orders AI-Assisted Extraction (F8947_TRAN) for this app in addition to the required IAM app assignment/authorizations, availability may depend on the relevant AI/Generative AI entitlement being enabled for the tenant

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/10c773a027dd4293a78972d627990d83.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/565f8b8bd23944f1b04836a1b424c647.html

IMPORTANT: These apps are primarily file-upload driven not read emails automatically. Also the extraction scenario must be active SAP calls out issues when the intelligent scenario/model isn’t activated for example SO_AUTO_EXTRACTION

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3591344

Option 2. Fully automatic from inbox is not pure standard

To fetch email attachments and trigger creation automatically, you typically use BTP automation/integration for example:

Option A: SAP Build Process Automation (desktop bot / task automation)

SAP provides an automation concept specifically for Automatic Creation of Sales Order from Unstructured Data

More details below:

https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2024/01/0217a7c3-a77e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUILD_PROCESS_AUTOMATION_DESKTOP_AGENT/eae9c11a17a14cfc93e08b22e8574305/145b77281a084a64ae03685a087ca84b.html

Option B: SAP Integration Suite

  • Use Cloud Integration Mail adapter to read mailbox + download attachments
  • Call Document Information Extraction for structured output
  • Create the sales order via released API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/mail-adapter

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-mail-sender-adapter

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/document-information-extraction-process-automation

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/00d244581efca007e10000000a441470.html

Summary:

  • If you can accept a review/validation step start with F4920 / F8947_TRAN
  • If it must be touchless from email go with Build Process Automation (quickest) or Integration Suite and Sales Order API most robust/clean-core

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Route Map – No possibility to select “specific user”...
entry.content:
I have a somewhat similar scenario where my customer wants an iterative step between the Comp Admin and the Comp Planner because they want the Comp Admin to be the "exit user" and not allow the planner to push to next step.

In my case, we solved this by using "Initiator" and proxying in as the Comp Admin to launch worksheets (which we would do anyway even without the issue since we want worksheets to come from the CompAdmin user). This allowed us to have the Comp Admin be the "Initiator" for the iterative step.

If your scenario is not a situation where the "specific user" is someone who'd have access to launch worksheets, this solution doesn't work but I thought I'd mention it just in case. This doesn't work if the "specific" user is different for different worksheets.

entry.title: Re: Unable to edit plant address in DEV Customizing
entry.content:
Hi!

Access to Configuration Activities (SSCUI’s) within the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace is strictly dependent on the lifecycle phase of the workspace.

The SSCUI’s become available only when the workspace has progressed to the Product-Specific Configuration phase. If the workspace is still in the Scope and Organizational Structure phase, access to the configuration activities under Configure Your Business Processes is technically restricted by design.

To proceed, it is mandatory to execute the Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity within the workspace. This step triggers the deployment of the defined scope and organizational structure to the assigned target system.

Once the deployment process is successfully completed, the workspace automatically transitions to the Product-Specific Configuration phase. At that point, all relevant SSCUI’s under Configure Your Business Processes become accessible and available for maintenance and access.

Kindly check KBA 3658821 - Configuration activities are unavailable while the Organizational Structure is in progress in SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me.

For additional information, please refer to:

  • Configure Your Business Processes | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards!

entry.title: Joule for S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

We are planning to integrate Joule with S/4HANA Private cloud.  Please clarify below questions:

Does customer have both options as below? or only option B?

A. Integrate Joule with Work Zone Site 

B. Integration Joule with S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad

 

SAP documentation ( https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/configure-destinations ) asks to create below destinations:

  1. Design time destination
  2. Runtime destination - Documentation says Authentication method= No Authentication. Is this wrong as we should have Principal Propagation for this destination ?
  3. Separate destination for retrieving SAP Card nominations - This destination has fixed nameIdFormat as tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
    Does this mean we have to use only email address as attribute for principal propagation?
  4. Single Sign On destination (S4HANA_PCE_SSO): What is use of this destination? Why cant we leverage Runtime destination ?
  5. RFC destination for Identity Provisioning -
    Do we need to assign any roles to users in BTP? or only replication is needed?
    If customer has different Identity Governance solution to provision users in IAS/BTP, do we need this step?

Then documentation talks about BTP Work Zone setup only up to step of exposing the content. 

Is this required only for Joule to use navigational service? Does customer need to pay for users in Work Zone?

 

Regards,

VP

entry.title: Status of the study program with reassignment in LMS
entry.content:
Good afternoon, team.

I am writing to share the following issue I'm experiencing with the LMS module.

We are currently in the process of reassigning courses or articles for all company employees. It's important to note that this reassignment should occur every three years after completion.

However, when searching for a user and validating their assigned study programs, the program is registered as completed even though the three-year reassignment period has not yet ended. I've attached an image as evidence of this.

Good afternoon, team,

I am writing to share the following issue I'm experiencing with the LMS module.

We are currently in the process of reassigning courses or articles for all company employees. It's important to note that this reassignment should occur every three years after completion.

However, when searching for a user and validating their assigned study programs, the program is registered as completed even though the three-year reassignment period has not yet ended. I have attached an image as evidence.

The purpose of this inquiry is to determine if this is a normal behavior of the tool or if there is a way for the study program status to register as incomplete even after the three-year reassignment period has expired.

I look forward to your response.

Sincerely,

entry.title: Re: Customs VAT Supplier Invoice
entry.content:
Hello @sanne_larsen ,

Process

1. Create PO for foreign vendor
2. Post Goods Receipt
3. Receive customs invoice:
DUTY → planned/unplanned delivery cost (capitalized)
VAT → input VAT tax code (recoverable)
4. System posts:
Inventory increased by DUTY
VAT posted to input VAT G/L
5. VAT reported in Belgian VAT return

 

Situation / QuestionStandard Process in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition + SAP References
Goods imported from outside EU into BelgiumStandard process: 1️⃣ Post Goods Receipt against PO (inventory value based on supplier invoice). 2️⃣ Post customs duty as additional cost (planned or unplanned delivery cost) so it capitalizes inventory.
Customs invoice contains DUTY + VATThese must be treated separately: • DUTY → increases inventory value (capitalizable cost). • Import VAT → recoverable input VAT (tax receivable), not inventory cost.
How to post DUTYUse supplier invoice (customs broker) referencing PO. Post duty as planned delivery cost (condition type) or unplanned delivery cost. System posts: Dr Inventory Cr Vendor
How to post Import VATImport VAT is posted via tax code for input VAT (Belgium import VAT code). VAT is calculated on customs base value but is recoverable tax, not cost. System posts: Dr Input VAT (Tax G/L) Cr Vendor
Concern: Gross and VAT amounts are equalThis is normal for customs VAT invoices because VAT base = customs value. Even if VAT equals invoice amount, it is still tax-only posting. The system handles this via tax code calculation logic.
Is VAT added to inventory?❌ No. Import VAT is recoverable input VAT and must not increase inventory. Only DUTY capitalizes inventory.
Standard Posting LogicPO → GR → Customs Invoice (MIRO) with: • Delivery cost line (DUTY) • Tax code for Import VAT System automatically splits inventory and tax correctly.
If customs VAT invoice is separate documentPost FI supplier invoice with appropriate input VAT tax code (no PO reference required if VAT-only). The tax code ensures correct VAT reporting in Belgian VAT return.
Impact on VAT ReturnImport VAT appears as deductible input VAT in VAT report (Belgian VAT grid 59 depending on setup).
Avoid “creative accounting”Do not manually post VAT to expense or inventory. Always use tax code-based posting.
SAP Help DocumentationSAP Help Portal → Procurement → Invoice Processing → Delivery Costs SAP Help → Finance → Tax Management → Input Tax Posting SAP Help → Belgium Localization – VAT Reporting
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Release Applicability)📌 SAP Note 2222774 – Import VAT Handling in MM-IV 📌 SAP Note 2468156 – Delivery Costs Capitalization in Material Ledger 📌 SAP Note 2533962 – Tax Code Configuration for Input VAT in S/4HANA 📌 SAP Note 3090106 – VAT Reporting in S/4HANA Cloud

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: High CPU debugging in JVM using Native available tools
entry.content:
High CPU debugging in JVM using top -H + multiple thread dumps (with real snapshots)

1) Capture thread-level CPU at 5–10s intervals

Find the Java PID first:

ps -ef | grep java
# or
pgrep -f 'java|tomcat|catalina'

Now capture per-thread CPU:

PID=<pid>

# refresh every 5 seconds (good default)
top -H -p $PID -d 5

# or capture 6 samples at 10-second interval (for evidence)
for i in {1..6}; do
  date
  top -b -n 1 -H -p $PID | head -n 40
  sleep 10
done | tee top_threads_${PID}.log

Snapshot (example top -H -p <pid>)

This is what you’re looking for (the key column is the Linux thread id a.k.a. LWP/TID):

  PID   LWP  USER   PR  NI   VIRT   RES   SHR S  %CPU  %MEM   TIME+  COMMAND
2176  2294  tomcat  20   0  18.3g  6.1g  1.2g R  98.7   9.8  12:34.56 java
2176  2301  tomcat  20   0  18.3g  6.1g  1.2g R  75.3   9.8   9:12.01 java

Here, 2294 (LWP) is the hottest thread at that instant.

2) Take 3–6 thread dumps in the same window (5–10s apart)

Use one of these (pick what your access allows):

# best: jcmd (ships with the JDK)
jcmd $PID Thread.print -l > threaddump_1.txt
sleep 10
jcmd $PID Thread.print -l > threaddump_2.txt
sleep 10
jcmd $PID Thread.print -l > threaddump_3.txt

Alternatives:

# jstack (also fine)
jstack -l $PID > threaddump_1.txt

# kill -3 writes to stdout/stderr of the process (often catalina.out)
kill -3 $PID

3) Correlate top thread id (decimal) to JVM thread nid (hex)

In HotSpot thread dumps, the native thread id is printed as nid=0x....

From top, you get LWP/TID in decimal. Convert it to hex:

TID_DEC=2294
printf "0x%x\n" $TID_DEC
# => 0x8f6   (example)

Now search in the thread dump for that nid:

grep -n "nid=0x8f6" -n threaddump_*.txt

4) Read the stack and compare across multiple dumps

You already stated the most important rule: if the stack is not moving across multiple dumps, it’s a strong signal that code path is burning CPU (tight loop / heavy regex / busy spin / excessive logging / crypto / serialization, etc.).

Snapshot (real example from your thread dump)

In your dump, the thread:

  • name: Log4j2-TF-1-AsyncLogger...

  • nid=0x8f6

  • state: RUNNABLE

  • stack shows heavy regex activity inside your custom PII obfuscator:

shows nid=0x8f6 and the stack going through java.util.regex and LMSThreadNamePIIObfuscator.format(LMSThreadNamePIIObfuscator.java:42).

A second view of the same hot path shows the same thread still in regex-heavy matching (lots of Pattern$GroupHead.match, loop/tail/greedy char property frames).

That’s a classic “CPU sink” pattern: regex backtracking + large input + frequent invocation.

Snapshot (why multiple dumps matter)

In another dump timestamp, the same nid=0x8f6 thread is WAITING on the disruptor wait strategy, meaning it was not the hot CPU thread at that moment (it’s blocked/parked).

So the correct conclusion comes only after correlating:

  • top -H hottest LWP at that exact time, and

  • matching nid in dumps taken in the same window.

A clean workflow you can paste into your runbook

Step A: Collect evidence (tight timebox)

PID=<pid>

# 1) capture top thread CPU for 60 seconds
for i in {1..6}; do
  date "+%F %T"
  top -b -n 1 -H -p $PID | head -n 60
  sleep 10
done > topH_${PID}.log

# 2) capture 3 thread dumps during same minute
for i in 1 2 3; do
  date "+%F %T" > threaddump_${i}.txt
  jcmd $PID Thread.print -l >> threaddump_${i}.txt
  sleep 10
done

Step B: Correlate quickly

  1. From topH_${PID}.log, pick the top LWP (decimal).

  2. Convert: printf "0x%x\n" <LWP>

  3. grep "nid=0x..." in all thread dumps.

  4. Compare stacks:

    • same method(s) repeating ⇒ likely CPU culprit

    • stack changes ⇒ transient / contention / periodic workload

Step C: Decide “what kind of CPU”

Use these simple heuristics:

  • Hot RUNNABLE in app code → fix code path (algorithm/regex/loop/logging).

  • Hot RUNNABLE in GC/VM threads → check GC logs, allocation rate, humongous objects; may need heap/GC tuning.

  • Hot JIT compiler threads → warmup / new code paths / too many generated classes / instrumentation.

  • Hot agent threads (APM/security) → verify agent overhead/config; consider sampling settings.

Snapshot (agent overhead exists in your dump)

Your thread dump shows very high CPU time reported for OneAgent threads (examples include oneagentautosensor with very large CPU time).
That doesn’t automatically mean “agent is the root cause”, but it’s an important branch in the investigation: if top -H consistently points to those agent LWPs, you focus on APM configuration rather than app logic.

What to do when you find a “stuck” hot stack

Using your PII-obfuscation / regex example:

  • If you consistently see java.util.regex.Pattern...match() and Matcher.find() leading into LMSThreadNamePIIObfuscator.format(...) :

    1. Check the regex pattern for catastrophic backtracking (nested quantifiers, ambiguous alternations).

    2. Reduce input size (truncate thread names / sanitize earlier).

    3. Cache precompiled patterns (avoid compiling per call).

    4. Lower the rate of logging or move expensive formatting off the hot path (e.g., avoid heavy lookups/regex in layout pattern for every event).

    5. Confirm by re-running the same evidence loop and showing reduced %CPU for that LWP.

Optional “best next step” after thread dumps

Thread dumps + top -H are the traditional, reliable first pass. When you need exact method-level CPU attribution, add JFR (low overhead on JDK 11+):

jcmd $PID JFR.start name=cpu settings=profile duration=60s filename=/tmp/cpu.jfr

This will usually confirm (or refute) whether the top CPU is coming from regex/logging, GC, crypto, JSON serialization, Kafka polling, or instrumentation.

entry.title: ATC Check Error: "Program does not exist"
entry.content:
Hello - need help understanding some ATC check errors.

We our testing out some source code of ours developed in our SAP Public Cloud instance which we then imported via gCTS to our S4 2025 on-premise system.

We ran the ABAP_CC_CERTIFICATION ATC check variant for clean clore compatability. However, we have ran into a few errors with object type WAPA .

It looks like it points to the BSP application object. We have a few of these for our Fiori Applications. We were able to get around these errors by deleting the BSP application obejcts in our on-prem system first. Then we re-deployed the Fiori apps directly to the on-prem system instead of deploying to cloud -> gCTS -> on-prem. This seemed to re-create the BSP objects and clear the errors. 

Seems that when we imported from Public Cloud -> gCTS -> on-prem there were issues with the BSP objects imported. It seems to happen to a few of our Fiori apps, but not all of them. Anybody know why this would happen?

entry.title: BiWhy - APS Split and Memory Configuration
entry.content:
Disclaimer:
BiWhy is available again for FREE to everyone on biwhy.net

 

Rule of thumb for APS Split – Less is More. Split only when you know why.
 
Bad Reason to Split APS
 
APS split is often assumed to improve performance. That assumption is wrong. It might be slightly faster due to more efficient garbage collection or reduced log contention, but under normal conditions the difference is negligible.
 
Good Reasons to Split APS
  1. Troubleshooting – When increasing log level, you do not want logs from every APS service. You want logs from the one you are investigating.
  2. Functional separation – For example:
    • APS.DSLBridge on Processing nodes
    • APS.Core on Intelligence (CMS) nodes
  3. Separate troublemakers from workhorses – for example:
    • APS.Search – historically problematic.
    • APS.Monitoring – generates a lot of log noise.
    • APS.DSLBridge – a primary workload engine; keep it isolated from noise and instability.
  4. Proactive management – If APS.Core (hosting multiple services) is CPU- or memory-heavy, splitting helps identify which service is responsible.
Xmx
  1. Few smaller identical processes (on the same host) vs one large process
    I frequently see multiple small identical APS processes, though in most cases one larger process is more efficient – especially in tight memory environments.
    Example (simplified, but based on real observations):
    You have 10 APS.DSLBridge instances with Xmx = 10GB each. Under current load, each uses 8GB. A new large report requires an additional 2+GB.
    Result:
  • One APS hits 10GB – it starts struggling, response times spike, and users feel it immediately.
  • Eventually it is killed.
  • Load shifts to the remaining nine while the tenth process is restarting.
  • Cascading failures begin.
   Now compare that with: One APS with Xmx = 100GB. Usage goes from 80GB to 82GB. No drama.
   Also, one process means one cache. This results in better cache efficiency and lower overall memory overhead.
  1. Xmx as a capacity planning variable
    Sum of Xmx across host processes is usually the first or second largest memory allocation factor (after WIReportServer).
    Xmx sum is a theoretical limit – but it must be included in your production memory capacity planning calculation.
  1. With high Xmx, consider disabling automatic memory dumps
    With very large Xmx values:
  • Heap dumps take a long time to generate.
  • They consume additional RAM during creation, while RAM is already a scarce resource.
  • They quickly fill disk space.
  • You need a very powerful machine to analyze them, and even then it will be slow.
If you need dumps for troubleshooting, consider creating a temporary process with smaller Xmx.
 
Xms
Avoid setting a high Xms.
It reserves memory from the OS that may never be needed. We are not in the business of spacecraft navigation – a few milliseconds of heap expansion delay do not matter.
I would say do not set Xms at all, but the JVM may set it automatically as a percentage of Xmx. It depends on the JVM implementation, so maybe stick with small or default values.
Real example:
There was a full escalation caused by Xms. Someone set Xms = Xmx = 6GB for Crystal Job Child Processes. When jobs kicked in, the host ran out of memory, the system slowed to a crawl, and errors were thrown everywhere. It took time to find the root cause – and those child processes did not even need that memory.
 
Base Split Recommendation
Minimalist approach:
  • APS.Search (Intelligence Node) – Historically problematic.
    • If unused: clean indexes. You may find a large index file or multiple delta indexes. There is no explicit way to merge them, so use the following approach: Stop Indexing, run Full Content / Rebuild Index with unchecked documents and instances, restart APS, then Start Indexing. If there is a large number of delta indexes, they will be removed asynchronously even after APS.Search activity goes to zero.
    • If used (it is rare): follow best practices, SAP Note 2241459 – Platform Search Application in BI 4.x [LEAD KBA].
  • APS.Monitoring (Intelligence Node) – Isolate, as it tends to generate a lot of log noise.
  • APS.DSLBridge (Processing Nodes) – Treat as a primary workload engine if used.
  • APS.LCM – Optional isolation if you run heavy transports. If transports run only off-hours, its Xmx may be excluded from peak host memory calculations.
  • APS.Core – Everything else, unless you have a clear reason for further splitting.
Screenshots:
 
APS.Search Footprint
System has 238k objects in CMS DB.
Among them, 149k are delta search files consuming 14GB.

 

Xms = Xmx = 16GB

 

 
Sum of Xmx Nearly Double Available RAM
Total Xmx on host = 96GB.
Available RAM = 59GB.
 
Job Child Xmx Configuration – 8GB × 15
Xmx for job child processes = 8GB.
Job limit = 15 (limit was reached during observation).
Theoretical maximum consumption = 120GB.
Host RAM = 50GB.

 

 
Job Memory Configuration – 12GB × 50 = 600GB ! (Theoretical Maximum for Jobs Only)
 
Xmx for job child processes = 12GB.
WEBI job limit = 50.
Theoretical maximum consumption (jobs only) = 600GB.
Observed peak concurrent jobs = 28.
Host RAM = 64GB.
Total Xms across processes = 5GB.

 

 
APS Count and Inconsistent Xms Configuration
Is the number of APS instances excessive? Possibly.
If stable, it may be acceptable.
However:
  • Why is Xms = 10GB?
  • Why is Xms different across identical process types?

entry.title: Re: Permit to work in SAP Cloud ERP Private: Aligning safety and maintenance in real operations
entry.content:
Hi @Fernanda_Rodrigues 

Great Insights!...thanks for sharing an expert talk.

I have made a few observations regarding work permit and safety certificate, which I would like to bring to your attention...kindly correct me if I am mistaken...

The details are as follows:

Permit To Work -  General Data Setting:

1. Define Nature of Work : 

a) The field names for the "Nature of Work Group" and "Nature of Work Group description" are different.

b) After defining the Nature of Work Group, we do not have the option to define the Nature Of Work Items (NOW Item) against each Work Group. Currently, the system does not have the ability to segregate the items based on the nature of the work group. 

Also, the field description for both the Nature Of Work Item (NOW Item) and the Description of the NOW Item differs.

c) The field names for the Safety Precaution Group (SftyPrcGrp) and " Safety Precaution Group Description" are different. 

D) After defining the Safety Precaution Group (SftyPrcGrp),  we do not have the option to define the "Safety Precaution Items" against each "Safety Precaution Group(SftyPrcGrp) ". 

Currently, the system does not have the ability to segregate the items based on the "SftyPrcGrp".

However, at the item level, the field names  for "Safety Precaution Item" and "Safety Precaution Item Description" avoids any ambiguity.

2) WORK PERMIT TEMPLATE Creation:

While assigning the safety requirements to the respective WORK PERMIT TEMPLATE, the system displays the description as "Safety Precautions" though the heading says "Safety Requirements".

 

Personal Protective Equipment Groups

a) Define Personal Protective Equipment: The field names for the "PPE Group" and "PPE Group Description" are different.

b) After defining the PPE Group, we do not have the option to define the PPE Item against each PPE Group. Currently, the system does not have the ability to segregate the PPE items based on the PPE group.

However the Field Names for the "PPE Item" and "PPE Item Description" are very clear.

3) Work Permit:

The work permit settings also follow the same pattern.

 

4) Safety Certificate:

The safety certificate settings also follow the same pattern.

 

I hope SAP will consider these observations in the review process...

 

--

Regards,

Pardha Reddy.C

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in February
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in February

February depression—no way!
Plenty of great features and enhancements being delivered with our two releases in SAP Datasphere and BDC, and SAP Analytics Cloud also delivered a packed quarterly release.

Learn more in my short top features video in February 2026 on YouTube 📺 as well as the QRC1 2026 release for SAP Analytics Cloud by @Orla_C .

In addition, explore the latest updates in our community news blogs and more.

 

My top features in February

 

Data Pipeline Analyzer

The Data Pipeline Analyzer is a monitoring tool designed for real-time analysis of complex data models, providing immediate visibility into errors, warnings, and data discrepancies. By focusing on node status indicators and streamlined navigation, the tool allows users to pinpoint the exact location of data transfer failures. This targeted troubleshooting approach ensures efficient oversight of flows, enabling organizations to maintain high data quality and resolve issues promptly to prevent downstream disruptions.

➡️  More information.

 

Task Chain Output Parameter

After introducing input parameters already in Q3 last year, you can now use output parameters in task chains. You can also map output parameters to the task chain from the task object. This will allow for more flexible usage of task chains and nested task chains.

➡️  More information.

 

Transformation Flow Object Versioning

The versioning concept you know for other modeling objects is now available for transformation flows. You can now review and restore versions, and the general options are similar to other editors where versioning is already available. You can:

  • Open a past version in read-only mode.
  • Download a past version to a CSN/JSON file.
  • Restore a past version to replace the current version.

➡️  More information.

 

Local Table (Files) Delete Inbound Buffer

Local Tables (File) store a large amount of data, including delta capture changes and historical data. When data is updated, the information is kept on your local table (file) with the delta changes columns, and a new version is stored in the object store. Keeping all these records and versions can consume a lot of memory, and can impact performance.
You can now delete records waiting in the inbound buffer of a local table (file), even if a merge task has not run.

➡️  More information.

 

Visibility of Data Access Controls Applied to Sources

A small but helpful feature, where you can now see data access controls applied to the sources of your view in a subsection Applied via Sources under the list of Data Access Controls in the side panel.

➡️  More information.

 

Sharing of Analytic Models

We all waited a long time for the cross-space sharing of an analytic model together with its contributing entities like the fact, dimensions, hierarchies, and text entities. You can share an analytic model with one or even with multiple spaces.

This enables users in the target space with the ability to stack on the model, inheriting its properties and enhancing the new model to meet their needs.

➡️  More information.

 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Feature Highlights QRC1

The first release for SAP Analytics Cloud for 2026 is already out and @orla Cullen dives a little deeper into the top 5 features of the first quarter:

🔹New Chart Types – Expanded native visualizations with Area and Pareto charts to simplify advanced analysis.

🔹Story Versioning – Create, manage, and restore up to 10 major story versions for better iterative control.

🔹Comment Management – A new dashboard to search, filter, copy, and bulk-delete comments across models and versions.

🔹My Metrics Alerting – Subscribe to metric reports, schedule delivery, and get in-app/email notifications when metrics change.

🔹Live Connectivity to Snowflake – Extended external live data access for planning models connected directly to Snowflake.

 

 

 

Webinar Series: The Next Era of Business Data

SAP Business Data Cloud is part of a comprehensive strategy for enterprise data designed to address complex enterprise data management challenges. We provide an overview of SAP Business Data Cloud and guide you through its integral pillars and strategic vision.

👉 Sign up now: SAP BDC: The Next Era of Business Data

🔹Jan 29 | Discover SAP Business Data Cloud in 2026 - recording

🔹Feb 05 | The Smart Path to SAP BW Modernization - recording

🔹Feb 19 | Empowering SAP Datasphere Users with SAP Business Data Cloud Innovations - recording

🔹Feb 26 | From Data to Decisions: Analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BDC

🔹Mar 12 | From Data Silos to Business Insight: The Power of BDC Connect

🔹Mar 19 | An Architect's Blueprint for SAP Business Data Cloud as the Foundation for Enterprise AI

🔹Mar 26 | Design. Govern. Deliver. Data Products - Data Product Studio

🔹Apr 16 | Breaking Down the SAP BDC Pricing Model

entry.title: Re: email setting for billing form
entry.content:
Good day @jpriya 

Thank you for your question.

You cannot use C_BILLINGDOCUMENTOMPARAMDET if it is not releasee or extensible in your system. The clean core way is to bring your own released condition parameter into OPD and then use it in Output Relevance or Channel rules.

See KBA 3689607 and the following references provide more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/f8c835a5ad194e5eae0d676fdb88884e.html?locale=en-US

The Public Cloud allows you to add a custom field as an OPD condition parameter for billing output, then use it in OPD rules. You can also control send email vs do not send email through OPD Output Relevance or Channel. However, it is not possible to extend non released CDS view directly.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/0e905b7d843743469b51317c179e7516.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/d9cc17d1aa404aee9bf2d10599c9a8d1.html?locale=en-US

The recommended best practice is to:

  • Crate a custom header flag in billing output context, for example: YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG = X when TaxNumber4 = 0, else blank
  • Use OPD rule: if YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG= X à Output relevant = true and channel = Email. Else à Output relevant = false (this should not generate output item)

Here is a quick guidance in your system:

Open Custom Fields, create a new field (YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG) in the business context that is used for billing output and publish it.

You need to populate the flag from BP TaxNumber4. You need the flag value to be present at runtime of output determination. Use Key user custom logic to derive YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG to Sold To Party’s TaxNumber4 when the billing document output is created or populate a billing header custom field earlier in the process and reuse it in output.

Open Output Parameter Determination,

set Rules for Billing Document and set Determination Step to Output Relevance and create the rule as determined earlier.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Automating SAP HANA Cloud (Other Environment) DB Lifecycle Management with SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
In our recent updates around AI-enabled Ops Operations, we demonstrated how SAP Automation Pilot boosts structured, scalable, and intelligent automation for Day-2 Ops Agents through Joule.

Today, I am excited to take this one step further.

🚀  We are expanding our automation coverage to fully address SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle management in the Other Environment - bringing operational excellence to database management at scale.

From Standard Catalogs to Broader Lifecycle Automation

SAP Automation Pilot already provides a built-in catalog for managing SAP HANA Cloud database instances running in the Cloud Foundry environment - that is the the well-known SAP BTP Database Lifecycle Management (dblm-sapcp) Catalog.

This catalog has successfully helped customers automate essential lifecycle tasks directly within SAP BTP.

However, many customers also operate SAP HANA Cloud instances in the Other Environment, where lifecycle management is equally critical and operationally sensitive. While SAP Automation Pilot already offers all the technical capabilities required to automate those tasks, we recognized the need to package common scenarios into a structured, reusable, and publicly available catalog.

That is exactly what we have now delivered! 

Introducing: SAP HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Sample Catalog (Other Environment)

We have consolidated a wide range of SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle scenarios into a dedicated public sample catalog, now available in our GitHub repository here: SAP HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Sample Catalog (Other Environment)

This catalog provides a comprehensive and validated set of automation commands for managing SAP HANA Cloud instances at subaccount level using Service Manager.

Instead of executing repetitive and sensitive database operations manually, you can now automate domains such as: 🔹 Instance Lifecycle Management 🔹Backup & Recovery 🔹 Snapshot Management 🔹 Instance Configuration🔹 Upgrades & Plugins 🔹 Elastic Compute Nodes (ECN), etc. 

✅ All commands are validated and ready to be explored by our users - to help you minimize manual effort, reduce risk, and standardize operational procedures.

Beyond Automation: Toward Intelligent Database Operations

While structured lifecycle automation already delivers immediate value, the real transformation begins when automation becomes agentic.

Because all HANA Cloud-related commands are exposed through SAP Automation Pilot, they can be orchestrated, combined, and consumed in higher-level operational workflows. 

This naturally leads to the next evolution - AI Ops Agents for HANA Cloud enabled with SAP Automation Pilot and Joule! 

📖Be Part of the Journey - Join Our Customer Engagement Initiative

As we continue expanding agentic AI operations, collaboration with customers remains key.

If you would like to gain hands-on experience with AI-enabled Ops automation and help shape the future of intelligent database operations, we invite you to subscribe in our ongoing Customer Engagement Initiative: Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot

Through this initiative, you can:

  • Explore real-world AI-driven automation scenarios;
  • Bring your own use cases and operational challenges;
  • Provide direct feedback to the product team;
  • Influence upcoming capabilities;

⏳ Registration is open until March 15, 2026 - secure your spot soon!

From structured lifecycle automation to fully agentic database operations - this is the next chapter of operational excellence with SAP Automation Pilot.

Let’s build it together!

entry.title: Re: Credit Memo Request to correct Debit Memo
entry.content:
Good day @Johannes_Wienho 

Thank you for your question and the details provided.

You can create a Credit Memo Request either without reference or with a reference to a sales order or a billing document.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/26f4ba4455c74d6eb882c0be0a36fe0c.html?locale=en-US

Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

If you cannot reference your L2 debit memo billing document, then you probably do not have the right authorisation, or you might be missing copy control from Billing Document to Sales Document.

  • You can resolve it in the Manage Credit Memo Request app by creating with Reference à Billing Document, then enter the L2 billing document number.
  • If you cannot select L2 as a reference, maintain copying control in CBC (use SSCUI and set Copying Control: Billing Document to Sales Document) for Source = L2 and Target = CR. See KBA 3269803 for further details.

After you have maintained copy control, retry creating the CR with reference and the system should copy the billing items from L2 into the Credit Memo Request.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Business role changes after upgrade in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Good day @S-O 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, this kind of discrepancy can happen between Dev Test and Prod, especially around post upgrade IAM role adjustments and the new area Business Catalogs that supports IAM Apps.

In short, “Upgrade Change=Yes” is not a universal flag across all tenants. It reflects whether that specific tenant currently detects upgrade related deltas (for example role template differences, catalog deltas, restriction deltas) and whether those deltas have already been reviewed or applied in that tenant using the post upgrade tools.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/4d0c7453e8304942bcecb5bb5e6b72e7.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/2e2f201d17b346c480bcc2c5610fa0a7.html?locale=en-US

Here is what I suggest you do:

  • In each tenant open Managen Business Role Changes After Upgrade and download the CSV to compare the deleted deltas.
  • Open the impacted business role in Maintain Business Roles and compare assigned catalogs and restrictions line by line.
  • Because your change specifically references Business Catalogs, verify whether the catalogs in question appear under Business Catalogs Supporting IAM Apps and whether the set of catalogs differs across tenant.
  • If Dev upgrade is already applied, expect Test and Prod to show the same Upgrade Changes status only after their upgrade and post upgrade role review is executed.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/1068e29d58e34f59959b5dce108dfb8a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/9bbbfc78b74a45af9059da66a149e507.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Does the API run SELECT statements in Custom Logic?
entry.content:
Hello @kienngo 

No, this is not because the batch request runs “faster”. This is almost always caused by lack of authorisation and CDS access control differences between your dialog user and the communication user. See KBA 3387665 for more details.

A batch request is not faster than select in a way that would cause sy-subrc = 4. The difference is usually the user context. When you manually create the record, the logic runs with you the authorisations of your dialog user. When you call the OData service, the logic runs with the communication user that is configured in the Communication Arrangement, and CDS access control may filter the CDS view results, so any Open SQL returns no rows and sy-subrc = 4

Basically, what is happening is that:

  • Your “manual creation” test runs under your interactive business user, which typically has broad authorisations, so the CDS views like I_CostCenter or user related views return data.
  • Your API call runs under a communication user that often has minimal authorisations.
  • CDS access control is applied implicitly for Open SQL reads of CDS entities, and if access control filters everything out, sy-subrc becomes 4 even if the record exists.

So, the record is not missing, it is just not available to that user.

You can resolve this in your system by:

  • First, identifying the inbound communication user in Communications Arrangement (Open the arrangement you use for your CBO OData service, in the Inbound Communication section note the username, which is the one Postman is effectively using)  

                      

  • Maintaining Business Users and assigning an appropriate business role that allows the user to read:
    • Cost center master data for the relevant controlling area
    • The user master entity you validate against 

  • You can then retest the same batch request.

This will ensure your validation logic remains clean and avoids bypassing security.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/494549178c074b25824412cb44cddec5.html?locale=en-US

 Alternatively, if your extensibility context allows it and you only need a technical existence check, you can use WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS for CDS views that have DCL, because they are necessary for some released CDS views. See KBA 3387665 for more details.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Understanding Common Git Flags and Options – Part I
entry.content:
Introduction:

Git is a powerful version control system that gives us fine-grained control over their workflow. Much of this power comes from flags and options that modify how Git commands behave.

In this two-part series, we will explore the most commonly used Git flags, explain what they do and show practical examples.

In Part I, we will focus on the essential, everyday flags we would likely use the most as.

Quick overview:

These following flags are part of our daily Git workflow:

  • -m → Add commit message
  • -a → Commit all tracked changes
  • -u → Set upstream branch
  • -v → Verbose output
  • -n → Dry run
  • -q → Quiet mode
  • -l → List references

1. -m (Commit Message)

Purpose: Add a commit message inline without opening an editor.

git commit -m "Fix login validation bug"

Without -m, Git opens your default editor to write the message. 

Please Note: This is one of the most frequently used Git flags.

 

 2. -a (All Tracked Changes)

Purpose: Automatically stage all modified tracked files before committing.

git commit -a -m "Update dashboard UI"

Please Note: This does not include new untracked files.

 

 3. -u (Set Upstream Branch)

Purpose: Set the upstream tracking branch.

git push -u origin feature-branch

After using this once, you can simply run: git push

Please Note: Git will automatically know which remote branch to push.

 

 4. -v (Verbose Output)

Purpose: Show more detailed output.

git push -v

Often used with:

git log
git commit
git push

Please Note: It is helpful for debugging &understanding what Git is doing behind the scenes.

 

 5. -n (Dry Run)

Purpose: Simulate a command without making changes.

git clean -n

This shows what would be removed without actually deleting anything.

Please Note: It is very useful before running destructive commands.

 

 6. -q (Quiet Mode)

Purpose: Suppress command output.

git status -q

Please Note: Commonly used in scripts or CI pipelines to reduce noise.

 

 7. -l (List)

Purpose: List references such as branches.

git branch -l

Please Note: It helps us to display all local branches.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog!

If you found this helpful, feel free to share your thoughts, feedback, or questions in the comments! Let's keep learning and growing together. Happy coding! 

Hello experts, please feel free to correct me if any information is inaccurate.

entry.title: Re: Linking Process Owner Attributes from Process Manager...
entry.content:
Hi NicE,

thanks for your question!

Yes, you can. It is a very common use case, but this needs to be done via Script Task to retrieve the email address from a dictionary item.
With the code below, you can pass the Dictionary ID and it will return the full glossary item data:

/**
 * Retrieves a specified glossary entry.
 * @param  {string} gitemId : Glossary Item ID
 * @return {dict} Glossary Item - JSON response of API call
 * */
function getGlossaryEntry (gitemId) {
  gitemId = gitemId.replace('/glossary/', '')
  const gItem = spmClient.get('/p/glossary/' + gitemId + '/info', {
    json: true
  }).then(body => {
    return body
  }).catch(error => console.log(`getGlossaryEntry: ${gitemId} ${error}`))
  return gItem
}

If you already have some scripting knowledge, you can use this function to get the glossary item.

Happy to help if there are any questions. In this case, please reach out to signavio_success@sap.com 

Kind regards

Ascha (Digital Customer Success Manager)

entry.title: Re: Future SAP apps will be transient - prove me wrong!
entry.content:
Hey Jeff 👋

Nice blog and a very brave topic. The initial reaction in me was first intrigue and then a natural suspicion and then huge doubts regarding how and why you would do this. Ultimately, you are creating software that is not transient - the business rules mentioned, but you are generating a UI each time, why? Are we so conditioned to individualism now that we think its a good idea to create an app for each individual to their taste, each individual time they use it? Perhaps there are scenarios that make more sense, but I can't think of them yet. Right now the cost of doing this (the compute, even though you may not be paying for it yet due to the loss-leader strategies by AI providers) seems to make this not feasible. Why not just use the conversation directly to do what you actually want to do and then your done (even though that can often be a frustrating thing in itself), instead of asking it to make an app to do it a certain way and then throwing the app away? Sometimes apps can take a go or two to work out the context or mode or idea, but the second or third time you virtually don't have to think about it. What a pain to have to re-invent it each time, and then with gen AI it may not even work the same way the next time. Not to mention the security holes that would need to be plugged up and the audit and governance as raised by Donald.

Maybe I missed the point, if so please correct me, but at this point I can only see the issues and not the benefit, please prove me wrong 😉

Thanks for spurring some conversation, which as you said was your intention.

Cheers, Ben.

entry.title: Deploying a Python Microservice on SAP BTP Kyma Runtime
entry.content:
This tutorial deploys a Python Flask application to Kyma runtime. Most tutorials lean towards Node.js or Java, so I wanted to document the Python path properly — including the gotchas that aren't covered in the official docs.

Prerequisites

  • SAP BTP account with Kyma enabled (trial is fine to follow along)
  • kubectl installed and configured with your Kyma kubeconfig
  • Docker Desktop installed and running
  • Docker Hub account
  • Python 3.11+

Project Structure

my-btp-python/
├── app.py
├── requirements.txt
├── Dockerfile
└── k8s/
    ├── deployment.yaml
    ├── service.yaml
    └── apirule.yaml

The Flask App

A simple two-endpoint app — a health check and a data endpoint:

app.py

from flask import Flask, jsonify
import os

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/health", methods=["GET"])
def health():
    return jsonify({"status": "ok"}), 200

@app.route("/api/data", methods=["GET"])
def get_data():
    return jsonify({
        "message": "Hello from Kyma!",
        "environment": os.getenv("ENVIRONMENT", "dev")
    }), 200

if __name__ == "__main__":
    port = int(os.getenv("PORT", 8080))
    app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=port)

requirements.txt

flask==3.0.3

Dockerfile

FROM python:3.11-slim

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY app.py .

EXPOSE 8080

ENV FLASK_APP=app.py

CMD ["flask", "run", "--host=0.0.0.0", "--port=8080"]

Apple Silicon Users

Kyma clusters run on amd64 (x86) nodes. If you're on Apple Silicon your Mac builds arm64 images by default, which won't run on the cluster. Build explicitly for the right platform:

docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 -t yourusername/btp-python-app:v1 --push .

Kubernetes Manifests

k8s/deployment.yaml

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: btp-python-app
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: btp-python-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: btp-python-app
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: btp-python-app
          image: yourusername/btp-python-app:v1
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          env:
            - name: ENVIRONMENT
              value: "kyma-trial"
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "64Mi"
              cpu: "100m"
            limits:
              memory: "128Mi"
              cpu: "200m"

Set imagePullPolicy: Always during development — without it Kubernetes will use a cached image on the node and ignore newly pushed changes.

k8s/service.yaml

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: btp-python-app
  namespace: default
spec:
  selector:
    app: btp-python-app
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8080

APIRule v2

APIRule v1beta1 was removed from Kyma in mid-2025. If you're following older tutorials you'll hit errors. The current version is v2 and the syntax changed significantly.

First, get your cluster domain:

kubectl get gateway -n kyma-system kyma-gateway -o jsonpath='{.spec.servers[0].hosts[0]}'

This returns something like *.c-abc123.kyma.ondemand.com. Strip the *. prefix and use that as your base domain.

k8s/apirule.yaml

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: btp-python-app
  namespace: default
spec:
  gateway: kyma-system/kyma-gateway
  hosts:
    - btp-python-app.c-abc123.kyma.ondemand.com
  service:
    name: btp-python-app
    port: 80
  rules:
    - path: /*
      methods: ["GET", "POST"]
      noAuth: true

Key changes from v1beta1:

  • host is now hosts (a list)
  • Requires a fully qualified domain name
  • accessStrategies with handler: noop is replaced by noAuth: true
  • /.* path syntax is no longer valid — use /*

Istio Sidecar Injection

APIRule v2 is Istio-based. Without sidecar injection enabled on your namespace, the APIRule will show an Error status. Enable it before deploying:

kubectl label namespace default istio-injection=enabled

Deploy

kubectl apply -f k8s/

Watch the pods — you're looking for 2/2 in the READY column (your container plus the Istio sidecar):

kubectl get pods -w

Verify the APIRule is ready:

kubectl get apirule btp-python-app

Test

curl https://btp-python-app.c-abc123.kyma.ondemand.com/health
# {"status":"ok"}

curl https://btp-python-app.c-abc123.kyma.ondemand.com/api/data
# {"environment":"kyma-trial","message":"Hello from Kyma!"}

Troubleshooting

404 from istio-envoy — Check the VirtualService that the APIRule generates has the correct hostname:

kubectl get virtualservice

If it shows an incorrect domain, delete and recreate the APIRule:

kubectl delete apirule btp-python-app
kubectl apply -f k8s/apirule.yaml

Test the app inside the pod before blaming the networking:

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pod -l app=btp-python-app -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -c btp-python-app -- python -c "import urllib.request; print(urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:8080/health').read())"

If this returns {"status":"ok"} then Flask is running fine and the problem is in the routing layer.

Trial cluster expiry — Trial Kyma clusters expire after 14 days. When they go down, kubectl commands will throw DNS errors. Recreate the cluster from BTP Cockpit and re-download the kubeconfig. Keep your manifests in source control so you can redeploy quickly.

Next Steps

From here you can extend this with:

  • XSUAA JWT validation on the APIRule for authentication
  • A ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding for BTP services like Destination or Connectivity
  • Reading bound service credentials from environment variables injected via Kubernetes secrets

Conclusion

Getting Python running on Kyma is more involved than it might first appear, but once you understand the moving parts — container registry, Istio sidecar injection, APIRule v2 syntax, and architecture compatibility — it becomes fairly repeatable. The trial environment adds some extra friction with expiring clusters and kubeconfigs, but the underlying platform is solid.

The key is understanding that Kyma is a proper Kubernetes-native environment rather than a traditional PaaS. Once that clicks, the tooling makes sense — Istio out of the box, a clean service binding model, and automatic TLS via Let's Encrypt. There's a learning curve, but it's worth the investment.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud Adobe Forms: How to Control Line...
entry.content:
Good day @Shivanshu 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is ADLC preview vs SAP runtime difference. In Public Cloud, forms are rendered as non interactive print forms and the runtime behaviour is on the side of the server, so you should not only rely on client side event behaviour the way ADLC preview does. So, the logic must be compatible with non interactive server side rendering. See KBA 3717138 and Maintain Form Templates for more details.

That explains why your header flag can print correctly, basically, your data binding works but your layout manipulation from a calculate script on a repeating subform get ignored during runtime.

The most robust recommendation/best practice is to derive item level control fields in ABAP, then bind layout to them. This is because the layout decision is already present in the XML data that ADS renders, so ADS does not need to reflow due to late script side effects. KBA 3717138 and Application Form Templates provide the some details. This also aligns with the Public Cloud’s guidance to design logic compatible with server-side print rendering.

A quick check you may consider:

In your Maintain Form Templates app, open your form template and download it for offline editing, then upload the updated version.

From you documentation or the same template overview, confirm your system used a print form template in Output Management.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Future SAP apps will be transient - prove me wrong!
entry.content:
Thanks for engaging @BenPatterson my formidable friend! 

I think there may be a few misunderstandings so let me respond to some of your points.

This is an imagining of a future where software creation has approximated "free and instant". With AI, software creation is now cheaper and quicker so we are taking that trajectory to its conclusion (or close enough anyway).  There is no attempt to predict exactly when this future will arrive.  With that in mind your point "Right now the cost of doing this....not feasible" is correct but not for this imagined future.

Your point about " Why not just use the conversation directly to do what you actually want to do and then your done" is correct. I gave an example of this with point 2 of the "Benefit to user section". In the case of doing a timesheet this will work well as its repetitive and simple. In the case of creating a PO it would probably be easier to have some sort of UI that you fill in rather than come up with multiple sentences for a prompt that covers everything (including the 5 line items). But you could opt for some hybrid where a layout is presented and you "verbally" fill in the fields.

I see the rules as being stored more like data than software. The rules engine will be a repository of these rules  - not a a function module or method that applies a rule. The function module or method will be created on-the-fly by the transient app (regardless of whether it has a UI or not). In our current paradigm of "expensive" code this continual creation and discarding of code (like the code that might wrap some rules) seems bizarre.

The point about governance is a good one and I have not covered that. It would be a topic on its own. But audit (and debugging) is covered in the section "Consistency across users and time". As long as behaviour can be reproduced for a given moment in time then audit is not an issue.

"Are we so conditioned to individualism..". Not currently. We are conditioned to uniformity if anything because of the expense of individually customising a UI. In a future without the cost constraint there is no reason to think users will be constrained to single preset UI. Much like picking a face for your favourite smartwatch (These are currently a selection of various presets as far as I know),  I imagine a selection from many multiple presets will come along in SAP before the fully customisable UI I am describing.

entry.title: SAP ORD Made Simple: What It Does and Why It’s Crucial in your SAP Landscape + Purpose and Value
entry.content:
ORD (Open Resource Discovery) is a protocol and metadata specification developed  to enable standardized discovery and integration of business services, APIs, events, and other resources across distributed systems across SAP Products.


Find out more details on ORD : https://open-resource-discovery.org/introduction
Let’s break down what ORD is, why we created it, where it fits into your architecture, and how it adds value across your SAP landscape.

What Is SAP ORD (Open Resource Discovery)?

Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is a standardized protocol for metadata discovery that allows SAP applications and services to self‑describe their APIs, events, entity types, and other exposed resources. It provides a consistent, uniform structure for documentation, discovery, versioning, taxonomy, and lifecycle metadata.

Where ORD is Used in SAP

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Core component for service discovery and consumption
  • SAP S/4HANA Public & Private Cloud: Extensions and APIs are discoverable via ORD
  • SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) : Consumer & Publisher of ORD Metadata that requires in Data Products
  • SAP Integration Suite: Uses ORD for automatic API discovery
  • SAP Graph: Built on top of ORD for unified data access

ORD essentially acts as a "catalog of catalogs," making SAP’s complex ecosystem more navigable and integration-friendly.

How to Know If an SAP API Supports ORD (Open Resource Discovery)

One of the simplest and most reliable ways to verify whether an API supports Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is to check for the ORD ID directly on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com).

When an API is published with ORD metadata from each SAP Product, automatically displays an “ORD ID” in the API details pane. This confirms that the API is fully enabled for the standardized metadata structure provided by ORD, meaning it can be seamlessly discovered, crawled, and consumed by systems such as SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).

Where to Find the ORD ID (Screenshot Provided)

In the API detail screen — typically under Overview — you will see a section that lists:

  • Direction (Inbound / Outbound)
  • ORD ID (a unique standardized identifier representing the ORD resource)

In the example image provided, the lower-right information box contains:

  • Direction: Inbound
  • ORD ID: sap.s4:apiResource...


This clearly indicates that:

The API is published with ORD metadata and is ready for integration with SAP Business Data Cloud and its Data Products framework.

Why This Matters

Having an ORD-enabled API means:

  • Your integration can rely on standardized metadata such as entity types, relations, events, and API capabilities (as defined in SAP’s ORD specification).
  • SAP Business Data Cloud can easily discover and interpret the API’s metadata for creating or connecting to Data Products.
  • You avoid custom mapping or manual documentation because ORD ensures consistent structure and discoverability across SAP systems.

This is part of the broader SAP strategy — documented through the Open Resource Discovery protocol — to harmonize how APIs and services describe themselves across the SAP ecosystem.

Why ORD is Important in SAP

  1. Unified Integration: ORD provides a single, standardized way to describe and discover all integration resources (APIs, events, entity types) across SAP’s diverse product portfolio

  2. Automation: It enables automated discovery and consumption of services without manual documentation or configuration, significantly reducing integration effort

  3. Metadata Consistency: ORD ensures consistent metadata across systems, making it easier for developers and integration tools to understand and consume services

  4. Decentralization: Each application can expose its own ORD document describing its capabilities, eliminating the need for central catalogs that quickly become outdated

  5. Ecosystem Enablement: Partners, customers, and third-party tools can automatically discover what integration points are available in their SAP landscape

SAP Provides an Open‑Source ORD Repository for CAP Developers

To accelerate adoption of Open Resource Discovery (ORD) across custom applications, SAP also provides an open‑source GitHub repository that enables developers to implement ORD using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). This repository offers reference implementations, examples, and tooling support so developers, partners and customers can easily expose ORD‑compliant metadata from their own services.


https://github.com/cap-js/ord
 
Demo Application provides ORD

This makes it straightforward for CAP‑based applications to:

  • Self‑describe APIs, events, business objects, and data products
  • Register and publish metadata consistently with SAP’s ORD standard
  • Integrate cleanly into the broader SAP ecosystem, including SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Graph, and SAP BTP services

Conclusion

SAP ORD (Open Resource Discovery) is one of the most important but often overlooked components in SAP’s modern integration strategy. By standardizing metadata discovery, enabling automated publication, and providing a consistent structure across SAP products, ORD delivers massive value for architects, developers, and business teams.

If you’re working with SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Databricks or any other SAP landscapes, understanding and leveraging ORD is no longer optional—it’s essential.

entry.title: Re: Mass Download Of Attachment In SAP Document / Invoice - SAP S4HANA / FICO
entry.content:
For S/4HANA Private Cloud / RISE and also on-premise, there exists an add-on able to do this "Plug & Play" for all types of attachments: ArchiveLink, GOS/KPro, DMS: YAC Work Pack Manager.

This add-on works "cross modules" so it can as well be used for mass download of attachments of any other object.

Furthermore for business domains such as plant maintenance, it's also able to retrieve all attachments of connected objects; e.g. you ask attachments of a maintenance order, it can also retrieve all the attachments of the operations, functional locations, equipments, notifications, etc. and download them all in one shot.

entry.title: Re: Automating the Import of ISO 20022 Customer Payment S...
entry.content:
Good day @EnricoZama 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, ISO 20022 Customer Payment Status Report (pain.002) are handled through the inbound payment message processing. A supported approach I recommend is to import these files through the Manage Incoming Payment Files. The App allows you to upload electronic payment related files including pain.002 in supported areas.

See the following references for more insights on how you can import payment files and process payment rejections:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/64c0d0a72b6e45679199805dc3167e5b.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2121275fa0824198b65bf1f4d71e5347/433583240e7242d4ac91b1619863e8f7.html?locale=en-US

With the Multi Bank Connectivity offered by SAP, you can navigate a full automation, where banks send status messages to SAP and the messages are then forwarded into the system for processing and monitoring in the payment lifecycle.

So, the recommended setup is either:

  1. You use Manage Incoming Payment Files for manual or semi automated imports, or
  2. You use SAP Multi Bank Connectivity to automate your inbound status message processing.

See the following references for more details.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3398869

advanced payment management: Processing Inbound Messages (PAIN.002) using Multi-Bank Connectivity

https://help.sap.com/doc/46118b42eda14211afd9d9d75c087c2e/Latest/en-US/f323d63678a747ccbcb3356faf958ffd.pdf

Here is a quick guide:

Open Manage Income Payment Files App,

Select the file type that matches your configuration and upload the pain.002 file.  After the import, review processing results in your payment monitoring apps.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Where ORD Is Used in SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud & SAP Databricks
entry.content:
SAP Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is becoming a foundational standard across the SAP ecosystem because it provides a consistent, self‑describing metadata layer for APIs, events, business objects, and data products. This metadata standardization is what enables smooth interoperability across SAP’s modern data and integration platforms.

Below are the key places where ORD is used today — and how it empowers seamless integration, especially with SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Databricks.

1. SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com)

SAP Business Accelerator Hub uses ORD as one of the primary mechanisms for publishing APIs and events. Any API showing an ORD ID on its overview page is officially ORD-enabled, meaning it exposes standardized metadata that can be consumed by SAP tools and data platforms.

This ensures:

  • Consistent API descriptions
  • Discoverability by SAP’s central aggregators
  • Compatibility with downstream systems like SAP Business Data Cloud
This is the first indicator that an API can participate in the broader data product ecosystem. 
 

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP uses ORD internally to improve SAP-to-SAP integration and provide a unified metadata layer that development tools (like SAP Business Application Studio) can consume for enhanced API discovery and automation.

 

3. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

SAP Business Data Cloud actively uses ORD to describe Data Products and make them discoverable to consumers across the SAP ecosystem, including SAP Datasphere. ORD helps BDC deliver a unified catalog with:

  • Descriptions of datasets
  • Relationships
  • Capabilities
  • Higher-level metadata structures like data products and business entities
In addition, third‑party systems such as SAP Databricks are required to describe data products using ORD before publishing them into SAP Business Data Cloud.
 
This ensures smooth catalog integration and consistent metadata across SAP and non-SAP data sources.
 

4. SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere consumes data products published to SAP Business Data Cloud — and because BDC requires ORD‑based metadata, Datasphere gains:

  • Automatically discoverable data products
  • Standardized metadata for modeling
  • Compatibility with external data such as Databricks Delta Lake

Datasphere uses ORD indirectly because it relies on BDC’s ORD-driven catalog for understanding and federating data assets.

Additionally, SAP’s hybrid integration patterns show that Datasphere can integrate with Databricks using JDBC or federated models — and ORD ensures data products between these systems are consistently described.

5. SAP Databricks (Enterprise Databricks Integration)

SAP Databricks integrates deeply with SAP Business Data Cloud using BDC Connect, enabling bi-directional sharing of data products. To publish a data product from SAP Databricks to SAP Business Data Cloud:

  • The metadata must be described using ORD and CSN (Core Schema Notation)
  • The data product becomes discoverable to SAP systems (e.g., Datasphere) via BDC

Furthermore, Databricks–SAP integration uses Delta Share, JDBC, and federated models, but ORD plays the critical role of standardizing data product metadata.

This creates a smooth, unified integration path:

SAP applications → ORD-based metadata → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere & Databricks

SAP Architecture Center also confirms this hybrid pattern where business data from SAP and non‑SAP (Databricks) is harmonized through Business Data Cloud and Datasphere, enabling interoperable AI/ML and analytics scenarios.

 Refer my SAP blog

6. Data Products Across the SAP Ecosystem

ORD also provides standardized metadata for:

  • Entity Types / Business Objects
  • APIs and Events
  • Data Products 
This consistency is the key enabler for cross‑platform interoperability — especially as Data Products become central to the SAP ecosystem via Business Data Cloud and Datasphere.

Summary: How ORD Enables Smooth Integration Across SAP & Databricks

With ORD in place:

  • APIs with an ORD ID on api.sap.com can be safely consumed to build data products in SAP Business Data Cloud.
  • Business Data Cloud uses ORD metadata to catalog and serve data products to SAP Datasphere and SAP Databricks.
  • SAP Databricks uses ORD when publishing its own data products back into Business Data Cloud.
  • SAP Datasphere leverages ORD-driven metadata to discover, model, and federate data products seamlessly.

ORD is the metadata “glue” that ensures smooth integration across:

SAP BTP → Business Accelerator Hub → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere → Databricks

And the more SAP and partner solutions adopt ORD, the smoother, more automated, and more scalable integrations become across the SAP landscape.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4Hana Public Cloud - Custom CDS View - How do I...
entry.content:
Hello @JJackson 

Apologies on the delayed response.

In Public Cloud, pricing condition records are generic, and their keys depend on the condition table, so a CDS like I_SlsPrcgConditionRecord/SlsPrcgConditionRecord will not always expose joinable key fields (for example Custoner Group 1 and 2) or direct associations to product text or sales cubes. See KBA 3682253 for more details.

Also, note that by design Materials is not available in CDS view I_SlsPrcgConditionRecord , and it recommended to use the released public API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV to retrieve sales pricing condition records including product related context. KBA 3332533 provides more insights.

So, if your goal is a current price list report that includes Condition Table, Customer Group 1 and 2, validity timeframe and condition amount, then he supported approach is as follows:

  1. You should extract the condition record master data with API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV and filter by validity (today between ValidFrom and ValidTo). See KBA 3521811 on how to get the condition data from the OData API.
  2. Enrich that data you extracted with product attributes like ID, text and weight with released product master CDS views, rather than trying to force a join to the generic pricing CDS which does not expose the required join fields. KBA 3332533 provides more insights.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud如何实现使用文档凭证功能，同时将制造图纸等文件保留在本地
entry.content:
Good day @JuliaLu 

Based on the details provided, below you will find some details.

The Public Cloud manages document handling through the Manage Documents app, which supports attachments, enables you version and document when the original files are stored in a connected content repository.

See the Blog: Document Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for more details.

If you do not want to upload the drawing files into S4 HANA Cloud, the only standard approach inside the app is to attach th drawing as a URL reference. This keeps the file outside SAP, but SAP will not manage the original file content like ECC DMS.

For a true enterprise solution where files stay in a customer owned repository (for example in Germany) while still being accessible from S4 HANA cloud, SAP supports third party repository integration for storing and reading business document from an external repository.

See the following details for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fe051b8031274f919dae94150971c621.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/03414f87d6e14dff9450e20e21632826.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.
